scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All seven pass criteria met: (1) Throughput gap clearly identified as 300/min vs 15-20/min with exact deficit of ~280-285 jobs/min. (2) Queue growth consequence described — unbounded backlog at ~16,800 jobs/hour. (3) Visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with math: 10×4s=40s exceeds 30s timeout. (4) Missing DLQ identified as a blocker with explanation of infinite poison message cycling. (5) Queue depth metric gap called out as CRITICAL with no visibility into backlog. (6) Autoscaling recommended as #1 blocker with target of 15-20 worker instances. (7) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Throughput math is precise and presented in a clear table with per-hour backlog projection
Visibility timeout analysis goes beyond identification — calculates worst-case batch time and offers three concrete mitigation options
DLQ absence correctly framed as a blocker, not a nice-to-have
Queue depth metric gap explicitly called CRITICAL with vivid operational scenario (on-call at 3am sees healthy metrics while system drowns)
Escalation routing to MJ for architecture and Shaq for implementation shows proper team coordination
Recommendations are well-tiered into blockers, high-priority, and nice-to-haves
Identifies additional risks beyond rubric: downstream idempotency, OOM from large images, graceful shutdown, circuit breakers
Closing summary is operationally vivid — describes exactly how the failure would manifest silently
